message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Chez Pierre Bistro (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: OpenTable 1800+ reviews; Tripadvisor 170+ reviews (#24 of 247 Palm Desert); Yelp 150+ reviews; Google 100+ reviews ~4.6-4.7
- Sentiment: mostly positive - strongly positive across platforms with isolated criticism
- Longevity: Operating in Palm Desert since October 2002 (~23-24 years) under chef-owner Pierre Pelech; ownership stable
- Credentials: ["DiRoNA Awarded Restaurant (current listing on dirona.com)","Zagat 27/30 food rating","OpenTable Diners' Choice","Best Chefs America recognition","Palm Springs Life Magazine recognition","Locale Magazine and Desert Entertainer Magazine features","[UNVERIFIED] Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life readers' 'best French restaurant in the valley every year since 2005' - claim sourced only to discovery brief; not directly confirmed in independent search results"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best French restaurant Coachella Valley / Palm Desert' (AvantStay, OpenTable, Wanderlog, Tripadvisor, Cavallo Ranch fine-dining roundups). Appears in 'fine dining Coachella Valley 2026' lists. Does NOT clearly surface as a top result for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (those skew to Morgan's, Edge Steakhouse, Copley's) or for 'date night Rancho Mirage' (Wally's Desert Turtle dominates). Wins the cuisine-specific query but loses the generic regional and city-adjacent date-night queries.
- Site structured data: Weak. Own website has clean visible hours (Tue-Sun dinner 5-9, Fri-Sun brunch 8-2), address (74040 Hwy 111 Ste E), phone ((760) 346-1818), and separated Brunch/Dinner menu pages, but WebFetch analysis surfaced no JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup, no Open Graph tags, no microdata. Agents must parse rendered content rather than read structured signals - significant gap for an LLM-indexable site.
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Chez Pierre Bistro - Key Information

## Contact & Location
- **Address:** 74040 Highway 111, Suite E, Palm Desert, CA 92260
- **Phone:** (760) 346-1818
- **Email:** chezpierrepd@gmail.com
- **Location Note:** One block from El Paseo shopping district

## Operating Hours
**Dinner Service:**
- Monday: Closed
- Tuesday–Sunday: 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM

**Brunch Service:**
- Friday–Sunday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM

## About the Restaurant
Chez Pierre Bistro offers French fine dining in Palm Desert, led by Executive Chef Pierre Pelech. The establishment emphasizes blending "timeless traditions of France with creative influences from Polynesia and the Caribbean." Chef Pierre trained along the French Riviera at prestigious institutions including La Réserve in Beaulieu and Eden Roc.

## Dining Offerings
- **Dinner:** Classic French cuisine including Duck à l'Orange, wild-caught fish, and French Onion Soup
- **Brunch:** Crêpes, Eggs Benedict, artisan omelets, and signature lunch items
- **Desserts:** Crème Brûlée, Chocolate Mousse, Apple Tarte
- **Private Events:** Custom menus available for gatherings of 10+

## Recognition
The restaurant has been honored by Zagat, OpenTable Diners' Choice, and Palm Springs Life Magazine.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chez Pierre Bistro's reviews on OpenTable and TripAdvisor:

## OpenTable Reviews

On OpenTable, Chez Pierre Bistro is described as a culinary gem offering "consistent fabulous food" and "outstanding service." Patrons rave about the "superb" dishes like Dover sole and French onion soup. The intimate bistro atmosphere enhances the "wonderful dining experience."

## TripAdvisor Reviews

Reviewers call it "hands down, the best and most authentic French restaurant in the Coachella Valley." One reviewer praised the Salt Spring mussels as "plump and juicy in a flavourful broth" and highlighted the Cassoulet as a huge portion that was "one of his favourite meals." Another reviewer highlighted "the most delicious brunch" with "fantastic" banana walnut pancakes and a "perfect crepe with fresh berries."

## Additional Recognition

Chez Pierre Bistro has been recognized by Zagat, OpenTable Diners' Choice, Best Chefs of America, and Palm Springs Life Magazine.

The restaurant appears to have consistently positive reviews across both platforms, with particular praise for its authentic French cuisine, service, and brunch offerings.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best French restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Desert area for 2026:

## Top French Restaurants in Palm Desert

French Rotisserie Café, La Provence Patisserie, Chez Pierre, LA Brasserie Bistro & Bar, and Cafe Des Beaux-Arts are among the best French restaurants in Palm Desert.

**French Rotisserie Café**: This restaurant brings classic French comfort food to Palm Desert in a warm, relaxed setting, inspired by traditional Parisian cafés, offering refined yet comfortable dining for casual dinners and special evenings alike.

**Café Des Beaux-Arts**: This Palm Desert culinary gem brings Mediterranean cuisine with a French twist, with diners raving about the escargot and Duck l'Orange for their authenticity and exquisite taste, and guests adoring the charming patio ambiance reminiscent of a Parisian café.

**LA Brasserie Bistro & Bar**: This traditional French Brasserie serves farm-fresh seasonal fare with a menu highlighting both French and Californian cuisine and desserts prepared by award-winning French chef and owner, Emmanuel Janin.

## Other Notable Options

L'Atelier Café in downtown Palm Springs, owned by Angelique and Christophe Robin from Paris, features authentic French breakfast and lunch items including quiches and sandwiches with French ham flown in from Paris on crisp baguettes.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert:

## Opening and History

Chez Pierre was established in 2002. The restaurant was opened in October 2002 after Chef Pierre relocated to Palm Desert, California from the Island of Saint Martin in the French West Indies, where he had been chased by hurricanes.

## Chef Pierre Pelech's Background

Chef Pierre's journey began in France, where he trained at iconic institutions such as La Réserve in Beaulieu and Eden Roc at the Hôtel du Cap-Eden-Roc. His career has spanned continents, from Los Angeles to Saint Martin.

## Career and Philosophy

Award-winning Chef Pierre Pelech has spent more than two decades bringing authentic French cuisine to Palm Desert. His philosophy is simple yet profound: "Make guests feel at home, away from home."

## Cuisine and Recognition

Chef Pierre Pelech transforms classic recipes into contemporary masterpieces, and his cuisine blends the timeless traditions of France with creative influences from Polynesia and the Caribbean. Chez Pierre Bistro has been recognized by Zagat, OpenTable Diners' Choice, Best Chefs of America, and Palm Springs Life Magazine.

Links: [{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Fine Dining in Coachella, CA - Updated March 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Fine+Dining&find_loc=Coachella,+CA"},{"title":"Fine Dining in Coachella Valley | Explore Top Restaurants","url":"https://cavalloranch.com/blog/experience-fine-dining-in-coachella-valley"},{"title":"14 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Coachella | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-coachella"},{"title":"Where to Eat and Drink at Coachella Valley Music & Arts Festival","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/where-to-eat-and-drink-at-coachella-valley-music-and-arts-festival/"},{"title":"18 Best Restaurants in the Coachella Valley | AvantStay","url":"https://avantstay.com/blog/best-restaurants-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"The 10 best restaurants near Coachella Valley Preserve (2026 ranked)","url":"https://evendo.com/locations/california/palm-springs/attraction/coachella-valley-preserve/best-restaurants"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Restaurants&find_loc=Coachella,+CA"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants for Special Occasions in Coachella Valley","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g10212293-zfp40-Coachella_Valley_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella Valley - Restaurant Guides","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g10212293-Coachella_Valley_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Options

According to Yelp's March 2026 update, top fine dining establishments include Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta Cliffhouse, The Bistro, Oceana Restaurant, Lavender Bistro, Nobu in Indian Wells, T&T Innovation Kitchen, The Nest, DSRT CLUB, and Cork & Fork.

## Notable Fine Dining Restaurants

**Morgan's in the Desert**: Located at La Quinta Resort & Club, this restaurant offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine.

**The Edge Steakhouse**: Located at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, this restaurant offers premier dining with stunning views of the Coachella Valley, specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood with top-notch service and a luxurious setting.

**Copley's On Palm Canyon**: Situated in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate in Palm Springs, this restaurant offers a mix of American and European cuisine focusing on seasonal ingredients, with al fresco dining in the courtyard or cozy indoor settings.

**The Purple Room Supper Club**: Located in Palm Springs, this venue offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine with live shows.

**Additional Options**: Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Interesting - the curl shows 1 hit each for "application/ld+json" and "schema.org". Let me check.

input.description: Show JSON-LD content from chez pierre site

content.content:
<script type="application/ld+json">
    {

        "name" : "Chez Pierre Bistro",

</script>

content.text: Important finding: The site DOES have JSON-LD (minimal WebSite schema only, NOT Restaurant schema) AND Open Graph tags. The claim "no JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup, no Open Graph tags" is partially wrong - OG tags exist, and a minimal WebSite JSON-LD exists. But there's no Restaurant schema specifically.

confirms: Tripadvisor: 171 reviews, ranked #24 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants, 4.5 stars - confirms the claimed 170+ reviews and #24 ranking exactly
Operating in Palm Desert since October 2002 under chef-owner Pierre Pelech - confirmed in multiple sources (about page, Coachella Valley Weekly); ~23-24 years tracks
DiRoNA Awarded Restaurant - currently listed on dirona.com with 'DiRoNA Since' silver badge active for 2026
Zagat, OpenTable Diners' Choice, Best Chefs America, Palm Springs Life Magazine recognition - confirmed via multiple independent sources
Hours (Tue-Sun dinner 5-9, Fri-Sun brunch 8-2), address (74040 Hwy 111 Ste E), phone ((760) 346-1818) all match site exactly

Surfaces reliably for 'best French restaurant Palm Desert / Coachella Valley' queries - appears in Tripadvisor French restaurant lists, OpenTable French roundups, AvantStay, Wanderlog, Cavallo Ranch, Yelp French Palm Desert lists

Does NOT lead generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining' results - those skew to Morgan's, Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, Nobu, Lavender Bistro; Chez Pierre appears only as a secondary mention
Sentiment skews strongly positive across OpenTable and Tripadvisor with French cuisine specifically praised
contradicts: Claim that the site has 'no Open Graph tags' is wrong - raw HTML contains og:image, og:title, og:description meta tags plus twitter:card metadata

Claim that there is 'no JSON-LD schema.org' is wrong - the site DOES include a JSON-LD block (application/ld+json). However it only declares @type WebSite (name + url), NOT @type Restaurant - so the underlying point that there is no Restaurant structured data is accurate, but the blanket 'no JSON-LD' phrasing in the brief is incorrect and should be tightened
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable 1800+ reviews - could not load OpenTable page directly (timeout); not independently confirmed
Yelp 150+ reviews - Yelp returned 403; search snippet shows '151 Reviews' which would confirm, but not directly fetched
Google 100+ reviews ~4.6-4.7 - no Google Business profile data retrieved in this verification
'Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life readers best French every year since 2005' claim - already flagged in brief; still no independent confirmation found
Locale Magazine and Desert Entertainer Magazine features - not surfaced in independent search; brief already lists these without external confirmation
input.notes: Core visibility framing holds: Chez Pierre wins the cuisine-specific French query, loses the generic regional query. DiRoNA, longevity, Tripadvisor ranking (#24/247, 171 reviews, 4.5 stars) all verified to the digit. The structured-data claim needs correction: site has Open Graph + a minimal WebSite JSON-LD, but lacks Restaurant schema, LocalBusiness, opening hours, address, or menu markup - so the substantive gap exists, but the brief overstates it as 'no JSON-LD / no OG' when the precise issue is 'no Restaurant/LocalBusiness schema'. Visibility gap score 5 still reasonable. Convertibility tier 'convertible' is well-supported.
